title: Selections Object
description: Provides access to and control over the set of selected entities in the user interface.

methods.name: add
methods.description: Adds the entity to the set of currently selected entities. The user will see the entity become selected in the user interface.

methods.name: asArray
methods.description: Returns an array containing all of the current selections. This is useful in cases where you need to iterate over the set of selected entities but need to create or edit data as you process each one. Selections are fragile and creation and edit operations will clear the selections so you won't have access to the complete list after processing the first one.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: clear
methods.description: Clears the selection set so no entities are currently selected.

methods.name: item
methods.description: Returns the specified selection using an index into the collection.

methods.name: removeByEntity
methods.description: Removes the selections that are associated with the specified entity from the set of selected entities.

methods.name: removeByIndex
methods.description: Removes an item from the set of selected entities.

methods.name: removeBySelection
methods.description: Removes the specified selection from the set of selected entities.

properties.name: all
properties.description: Gets or sets all entities currently selected.

properties.name: count
properties.description: Gets the number of entities currently selected.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

add.title: Selections.add Method
add.description: Adds the entity to the set of currently selected entities. The user will see the entity become selected in the user interface.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity to select and add to this selection set.

asArray.title: Selections.asArray Method
asArray.description: Returns an array containing all of the current selections. This is useful in cases where you need to iterate over the set of selected entities but need to create or edit data as you process each one. Selections are fragile and creation and edit operations will clear the selections so you won't have access to the complete list after processing the first one.
asArray.return_description: Returns an array of all of the current selections. Selection objects are returned so you'll need to call their entity properties to get the actual selected entity.

classType.title: Selections.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

clear.title: Selections.clear Method
clear.description: Clears the selection set so no entities are currently selected.

item.title: Selections.item Method
item.description: Returns the specified selection using an index into the collection.
item.return_description: Returns the specified item or null if an invalid index was specified.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the item within the collection to return. The first item in the collection has an index of 0.

removeByEntity.title: Selections.removeByEntity Method
removeByEntity.description: Removes the selections that are associated with the specified entity from the set of selected entities.
removeByEntity.return_description: Returns true if the item was removed or not currently selected.

parameters.name: entity
parameters.description: The entity to remove selections of.

removeByIndex.title: Selections.removeByIndex Method
removeByIndex.description: Removes an item from the set of selected entities.
removeByIndex.return_description: Returns true if the item was removed successfully.

parameters.name: index
parameters.description: The index of the selection to remove.

removeBySelection.title: Selections.removeBySelection Method
removeBySelection.description: Removes the specified selection from the set of selected entities.
removeBySelection.return_description: Returns true if the item was removed or not currently selected.

all.title: Selections.all Property
all.description: Gets or sets all entities currently selected.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selections_var.all

# Set the value of the property.
selections_var.all = propertyValue

count.title: Selections.count Property
count.description: Gets the number of entities currently selected.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selections_var.count

isValid.title: Selections.isValid Property
isValid.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selections_var.isValid

objectType.title: Selections.objectType Property
objectType.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object. It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = selections_var.objectType
